_doc: Layer 2 of the screener's news ingestion (CLAUDE.md-versioned doctrine, not engine code): direct publisher RSS/Atom feeds, polled alongside GDELT (Layer 1) and the NSE disclosure API (Layer 3) every cycle. Items still pass the approved-domains firewall on their LINK domain — adding a feed here never bypasses Gate 0. Every URL was verified live (HTTP 200 + parseable by scripts/verify-feeds.ts, which reuses the production parseFeed) before being added. The fetcher uses a browser User-Agent by default (UA-sniffing publishers soft-block bots); per-feed `user_agent` overrides it where required — SEC/.gov feeds carry a descriptive contact UA. Known non-starters, checked and excluded: Reuters (no public RSS since 2020) and AP/AFP direct (only via Google News, which we drop to preserve Gate-0 link-domain discipline) — these still arrive via GDELT; Business Standard RSS (403 on every UA). NSE/BSE corporate filings come via the NSE adapter (sources/nse.ts), not RSS. To add a feed: verify it with `npx tsx scripts/verify-feeds.ts`, then ensure its LINK domain is on the approved-domains firewall.
